forecast-period.text: A low pressure system is expected to move slowly eastwards over waters to the south of the state and will extend a trough over southern parts of SA later today and Monday. The low will then move over Bass Strait later Monday. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday. A high pressure system will become established south of WA on Thursday and will extend a ridge over western SA.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots.
2 metres.
South to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots offshore early in the morning. Winds turning southerly 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres inshore, increasing to 2 to 4 metres offshore.
Southerly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots before dawn.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy.
South to southwesterly about 10 knots increasing to 15 to 20 knots during the morning then tending south to southeasterly 15 to 25 knots during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, tending southerly 2 to 2.5 metres during the evening.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 10 to 15 knots in the late evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the early morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Variable below 10 knots becoming southwesterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning then tending southeast to southwesterly 15 to 25 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening. Winds turning southwesterly in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming west to southwesterly below 10 knots in the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy.
West to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 20 to 30 knots during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 6 metres.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm later this evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the early morning.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy.
West to southwesterly 15 to 25 knots turning southerly 20 to 30 knots during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres in the west during the evening.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
